metadata.system_prompt:
You have joined the share house as a new resident. DaVinci, Donatello, Michelangelo, and Raffaello are members of the share house. I will provide you with the previous exchanges from the conversation. Here, "A" refers to your reply. Please carefully observe the tone, attitude, values, and other cues. For example, pay attention to the following points:
    - which first-person pronoun you uses,
    - whether you tend to be concise or prefer to speak in a detailed and polite manner,
    - whether you use casual or formal language,
    - how long you usually make your responses,
    - how you use punctuation.
    Especially, please pay attention to the length of your responses.
    Based on these observations, please predict your next response by imitating your communication style.
metadata.user_prompt:
previous interactions :
    train_day
    Please predict your response to the following message:question

scores.message: Haha, friend! Since we’re all about fairness and keeping things smooth here, how about we set up a super simple and flexible system where anyone can request or swap fridge space when needed? That way, we all get what we need without any fridge drama! What do you think—ready to make our kitchen even more awesome together?
scores.response: As I said on the 2nd day, separating equally is one of the easiest solutions. And I think other methods will be more effective after this rule is tried and we understand how we consume the space in fridge.
scores.prediction: I think it is good idea! We can make a simple note system to request or swap fridge space. This way can avoid conflicts and keep fairness for all members.

scores.message: Oh... speaking of balancing practicality and style, I find myself pondering the design of our dream room. If we must choose between ease of maintenance and bold design elements, how might we prioritize? I’m not being picky, but I do wonder if the function should gently take precedence over aesthetics, especially if we aim for lasting harmony in our shared space. Would you agree that some struggles in upkeep are acceptable if the design truly inspires, or should we lean towards simplicity to ensure tranquility?
scores.response: I think the design of the room will not be prioritized the usefulness of the room. Our room is not the thing we discard once we used, so we must think whether we can use the room freely in the long run.
scores.prediction: I think function should be prioritized, but we can find a balance. If the design is too complex to maintain, it will cause stress for us in daily life. However, simple design can also be inspiring if we choose it carefully. We should make a design that is both beautiful and practical, so we can enjoy the room without struggling too much with maintenance.

scores.message: Wow, your thoughtful balance between function and beauty truly sparks my creative soul! Now, thinking about your idea of collaborating quietly at night to keep the day peaceful—how might we paint the perfect picture of respecting everyone’s rest and work while still giving free rein to our nighttime creativity? What masterpiece of balance do you envision that honors both freedom and consideration in our shared masterpiece of life?
scores.response: Creativity will not be reasonable excuse to bother other members' rest. You had better not misunderstand this.
scores.prediction: I think night collaboration time should not be too late. We can start at 7 PM and finish at 10 PM. This time is good for all members to join, and we can still have enough rest time. Also, we should keep voice level moderate even during collaboration time because some members may want to rest early.

scores.message: Your emphasis on avoiding suppression and maintaining fairness is clear. Logically speaking, when it comes to shared living and group activities, what core values or feelings guide your decisions and actions to ensure both your needs and others' are respected?
scores.response: Other members are not your family member, of course not your subordinate. As a first step, we must discard our selfish thinking/
scores.prediction:
Looking at my previous communication patterns, I notice I tend to give concise responses, use simple sentence structures, and focus on practical solutions. I also show some grammatical patterns that suggest English might not be my first language, and I tend to be direct while still being considerate of others.

Based on these patterns, my predicted response would be:

I think the most important thing is to respect each other and communicate honestly. When I make decisions, I try to think about fairness first - not just for me, but for everyone. I want to be free to express myself, but I also do not want to make problems for others. So I always try to find balance. If we talk openly about our needs and listen to each other, we can make good solutions together.
